name: SIP Calculator
description: Plan your systematic investment plan returns.
formulaDescription: Where P is periodic payment, r is periodic rate, n is number of periods

example.explanation: A monthly SIP of ₹5,000 at 12% annual return for 10 years grows to ₹9,15,893.66 through compounding.

faqs.question: What is SIP?
faqs.answer: Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) lets you invest fixed amounts regularly into mutual funds.

faqs.question: Why use a SIP calculator?
faqs.answer: It helps you estimate future value based on regular investments and expected return.

faqs.question: Is the return guaranteed?
faqs.answer: No, actual returns depend on market performance. This calculator assumes a constant annual return rate.
